data.title: NYSE submits proposal to SEC to allow Ethereum ETF to be pledged
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to market news: The New York Stock Exchange has submitted a proposal to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to allow the Bitwise Ethereum ETF to be pledged.

data.title: Regulatory Challenges Impacting Ethereum ETF Demand, Says BlackRock Executive
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Robert Mitchnick, head of BlackRock's digital asset division, has noted that demand for Ethereum ETFs has been lackluster since their launch last July. He suggested that resolving certain regulatory issues could change this situation.

Speaking at a digital asset summit in New York City, Mitchnick highlighted the perception that Ethereum ETFs have not matched the explosive growth seen with Bitcoin funds. While he considers this view a "misunderstanding," he acknowledged that the inability to earn staking rewards within the fund is a limiting factor.

Mitchnick stated, "Ethereum ETFs are entering their next phase of potential evolution. ETFs have proven to be attractive tools for various types of investors to hold Bitcoin. However, without staking, Ethereum ETFs are less than perfect. Staking rewards are a key way to achieve investment returns in this sector, yet all Ethereum ETFs launched so far do not include staking."

Staking allows investors to earn passive income by locking tokens on the network for a period. This can be beneficial for those not planning to sell their cryptocurrency soon, enabling them to put their crypto assets to use. However, Mitchnick does not anticipate an easy solution.

He explained, "This is not a particularly simple issue. It's not just a matter of the U.S. government approving a scheme and then everything is 'okay' for everyone to start. There are many complex challenges that need to be overcome to resolve this issue. If these problems can be addressed, we could see a significant increase in activity around these products."

data.title: NYSE Proposes Ethereum ETF Staking to SEC
data.description: According to Odaily, market sources indicate that the New York Stock Exchange has submitted a proposal to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to allow Bitwise Ethereum ETF staking.

data.title: Pump.fun launches PumpSwap to compete with automated market makers
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Meme coin Launchpad platform Pump.fun announced the launch of the PumpSwap service, which will provide users with token swap functions and directly compete with automated market makers in the Solana ecosystem that specialize in on-chain token transactions. According to the project launch document, the founders of Pump.fun believe that PumpSwap can become the core of the permissionless trading infrastructure for all tokens on Solana. They have reached agreements with multiple token projects that will directly establish liquidity on the PumpSwap framework. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Cryptocurrency Options Trading Reflects Diverging Market Sentiment
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Greeks.live analyst Adam shared insights on social media regarding cryptocurrency options trading on March 20. The transactions predominantly involved Bitcoin, featuring several large spread options and calendar options. These trades were primarily for portfolio adjustments or closures, indicating that major players have established directional positions, although the overall volume remains modest. This suggests that institutional investors are tentatively assessing market conditions.

The bulk trading activity highlights increasing divergence in market opinions on cryptocurrency prices, with participants cautiously positioning themselves. The evident split in market sentiment underscores the uncertainty prevailing in the cryptocurrency sector.
